---
title: "Week 2 — Study design, bias, and causality"
toc: true
---

> **Status:** placeholder. Real content will be developed during the
> course build.

## Big question

Where did the data come from?

## Day-by-day

### Monday — concept

Observational studies versus experiments; how sampling and assignment
shape what we can conclude.

### Wednesday — micro-lab ticket

Short scaffolded task: a "study design autopsy" on a research summary.

### Friday — quiz / application

Quiz mirroring Wednesday's micro-lab on a new scenario, followed by a
clinical-trial-vs-records-study comparison.

## Primary source basis

OpenIntro IMS Ch 2 (data-design).

## Supplemental source / custom needs

Clinical and biological examples; ISLBS supplements help here once it
is mirrored.

## Learning goals

Students should be able to:

- [ ] Classify a study as observational or experimental.
- [ ] Identify random sampling and random assignment.
- [ ] State whether causal claims are justified.
- [ ] Name plausible sources of bias or confounding.

## Assessment links

Links to the bank/manifests for this week will appear here once items
are built.

- Micro-lab ticket: TBD
- Friday quiz: TBD
- Homework: TBD
